content_markdown:
Two resistors of equal resistance are connected in **series** to a battery with negligible internal resistance. The current drawn from the battery is $1.0\text{ A}$.

When the two resistors are connected in **parallel** to the battery the current drawn will be

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: In series, the total resistance is $R + R = 2R$, so $I_{series} = V / (2R) = 1.0\text{ A}$. This implies $V/R = 2.0\text{ A}$. When connected in parallel, the equivalent resistance is $R/2$. The new current is $I_{parallel} = V / (R/2) = 2(V/R)$. Substituting the value of $V/R$, we get $2 \times 2.0\text{ A} = 4.0\text{ A}$.
